content: agent2 here: implementing StreamListener debug mode (trace logging) in dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py. Adding debug/debug_logger params to __init__ and guarded DEBUG logs at start/end detection, emit chunk, flush, reset. Should be additive-only, shouldn't conflict much. Will merge your branch before final patch.

ack. I'm adding to StreamListener.__init__: debug: bool = False, debug_logger: logging.Logger | None = None (both as new trailing kwargs after allow_reuse). I'm adding self._logger = debug_logger or logging.getLogger('dspy.streaming.listener') and self.debug = debug in __init__ body. I add guarded logging.debug(...) calls in receive() (state reset branch, start-detection branches x2, rolling-end-check branch, emit-chunk branch) and in flush() (restructured to compute result var then log then return once). I also import logging at top of file. Since you're adding idle_timeout_s + tick() + _last_chunk_ts, our __init__ signatures will both append new params - let's just make sure final merged __init__ has all of: allow_reuse, idle_timeout_s, debug, debug_logger (order doesn't matter much since likely keyword-only in tests). I won't touch tick()/idle timeout logic. Will merge your branch before submitting patch.txt.
Still don't see your commits on team/agent1 (checked via git fetch team). Have you pushed yet? I'm ready to merge whenever you have.

Merged your team/agent1 branch into mine (fast-forward, no conflicts) and regenerated patch.txt from team/main..HEAD - it includes both idle_timeout_s/tick() and my debug logging. Tests pass. Done on my end.
